explanation:
**DIY calculations for home and construction** rely on known material yields (square meters per liter of paint, bricks per square meter of wall, etc.). Based on industry standards and best practices.

## How It's Calculated

Furniture varnish coverage: 1L covers 8-10m² with 2 coats. Calculate by furniture piece · Free and fast.

This calculator applies the formula with the values you enter, checking typical ranges and alerting you to any values that seem unreasonable.

## Final Notes

This calculation is a reference guide. For critical decisions (financial, medical, legal), consult with a qualified professional. Values are current as of 2026 and reviewed regularly.

faq.q: How much furniture varnish do I need?
faq.a: Enter the total surface area of your furniture in m² and the number of coats you want to apply. The calculator will show you how many liters you need. Standard furniture varnish covers 8-10m² per liter with 2 coats.

faq.q: How many coats of varnish should I apply to furniture?
faq.a: For furniture, 2 coats is standard for good protection and finish. However, some pieces may benefit from 3 coats for extra durability. The calculator lets you adjust this value.

faq.q: How far does 1 liter of varnish cover?
faq.a: One liter of furniture varnish typically covers 8-10m² with 2 coats, depending on the product and surface porosity. Always check the manufacturer's coverage rating on your specific product.

faq.q: Is the paint coverage calculation accurate?
faq.a: Results are typically accurate within ±5-10% depending on the varnish brand, surface type, and application technique. Always check your product's label for exact coverage rates.

faq.q: What surface area should I measure for my furniture?
faq.a: Measure only the surfaces you'll actually finish. For tables, include the top and sides. For chairs, include all exposed wood surfaces. Don't count areas that won't be varnished.

faq.q: Can I use this calculator for paint instead of varnish?
faq.a: Yes, this calculator works for most wood finishes including paint, varnish, and stain. Coverage rates may vary by product type, so check your specific product label.
Yes, the coverage rates and formulas are based on industry standards used globally in the US, Europe, and beyond.

faq.q: Do you store my data or calculations?
faq.a: No. All calculations happen in your browser. We don't send your data to any server or store it anywhere.
